{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Officer",
  "description": "Electra Global Logistics Limited is a logistics and freight forwarding company headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria. The firm positions itself as a strategic partner that provides &quot;intelligent infrastructure&quot; for businesses by managing the movement of goods across various sectors, including energy, pharmaceuticals, and manufacturing.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Officer

Job Location: Oregrun, Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are looking for a proactive Operations Officer to manage and optimize daily logistics activities.
	This role is ideal for someone who can coordinate supply chain processes, ensure efficient movement of goods, maintain accurate documentation, and keep operations running smoothly.
	You will be a key point of coordination, ensuring seamless execution across freight, clearance, and delivery functions.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate daily logistics activities including shipment processing, documentation, and tracking.
	Manage import and export handling processes, ensuring accuracy and timeliness.
	Prepare and verify shipping documents, customs documentation, and regulatory paperwork.
	Liaise with shipping lines, and internal teams to facilitate smooth operations.
	Monitor shipment progress and proactively address delays or disruptions.
	Maintain accurate operational records and update tracking systems.
	Identify process inefficiencies and recommend improvements for a better workflow.
	Support compliance with safety, legal, and company policies.
	Assist in cost control by monitoring operational expenses and resource usage.


Requirements


	2&ndash;4 years of experience in logistics operations, import/export handling, or supply chain coordination.
	Practical knowledge of shipping documentation, customs clearance, and regulatory procedures.
	Ability to manage multiple shipments and coordinate with various stakeholders.
	Strong attention to detail in documentation and record keeping.
	Good understanding of freight processes (air and sea).
	Familiarity with logistics tracking tools and operational systems.


Benefits


	Pension plan.
	HMO (Health Insurance).
	13th Month Salary.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-09",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-10",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

Operations Officer Job at Electra Global Logistics Limited




        
    

        
        Electra Global Logistics Limited is a logistics and freight forwarding company headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria. The firm positions itself as a strategic partner that provides "intelligent infrastructure" for businesses by managing the movement of goods across various sectors, including energy, pharmaceuticals, and manufacturing.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Officer

Job Location: Oregrun, Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a proactive Operations Officer to manage and optimize daily logistics activities.
	This role is ideal for someone who can coordinate supply chain processes, ensure efficient movement of goods, maintain accurate documentation, and keep operations running smoothly.
	You will be a key point of coordination, ensuring seamless execution across freight, clearance, and delivery functions.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate daily logistics activities including shipment processing, documentation, and tracking.
	Manage import and export handling processes, ensuring accuracy and timeliness.
	Prepare and verify shipping documents, customs documentation, and regulatory paperwork.
	Liaise with shipping lines, and internal teams to facilitate smooth operations.
	Monitor shipment progress and proactively address delays or disruptions.
	Maintain accurate operational records and update tracking systems.
	Identify process inefficiencies and recommend improvements for a better workflow.
	Support compliance with safety, legal, and company policies.
	Assist in cost control by monitoring operational expenses and resource usage.


Requirements


	2–4 years of experience in logistics operations, import/export handling, or supply chain coordination.
	Practical knowledge of shipping documentation, customs clearance, and regulatory procedures.
	Ability to manage multiple shipments and coordinate with various stakeholders.
	Strong attention to detail in documentation and record keeping.
	Good understanding of freight processes (air and sea).
	Familiarity with logistics tracking tools and operational systems.


Benefits


	Pension plan.
	HMO (Health Insurance).
	13th Month Salary.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "General Manager (GM)",
  "description": "Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited (formerly Moody International Nigeria Limited) was established in 1996 as a partnership between Moody International Group and Nigerian investors. Since commencing operations in March 1997 the company continues to grow and exerts its presence in numerous Projects in the Nigeria Oil and Gas Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager (GM)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary


	The General Manager is responsible for providing strategic leadership and overall management of the company&#39;s operations, business development, project execution, financial performance, and regulatory compliance.
	The role oversees all operational activities within the business units, ensuring projects are delivered safely, efficiently, on schedule, and within budget while driving profitability and business growth.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong leadership experience within the oil &amp; gas industry and renewable energy sector, with a proven track record of managing large-scale projects, business development activities, stakeholder engagement, and multidisciplinary teams.


Key Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership:


	Develop and implement business strategies, plans, and objectives aligned with the organization&#39;s vision and goals.
	Drive business growth initiatives, market expansion, and revenue generation opportunities.
	Monitor industry trends, market conditions, and competitor activities to identify business opportunities and risks.
	Provide strategic recommendations to executive management and stakeholders.


Operational Management:


	Oversee daily operations across all departments to ensure efficiency, productivity, and service excellence.
	Establish operational policies, procedures, and performance standards.
	Monitor operational performance and implement continuous improvement initiatives.
	Ensure optimal utilization of company resources and assets.


Financial Management:


	Develop and manage annual budgets, forecasts, and financial plans.
	Monitor financial performance, profitability, and cost-control measures.
	Review financial reports and ensure achievement of revenue and profitability targets.
	Approve expenditures and ensure financial discipline across the organization.


Business Development &amp; Client Relations:


	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, partners, regulatory bodies, and key stakeholders.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities, partnerships, and contracts.
	Lead contract negotiations and major business engagements.
	Ensure high levels of customer satisfaction and service delivery.


People Management:


	Provide leadership, direction, and mentorship to departmental managers and employees.
	Drive employee engagement, performance management, and talent development initiatives.
	Promote a culture of accountability, teamwork, and continuous improvement.
	Oversee workforce planning and organizational development activities.


Compliance, Risk &amp; Governance:


	Ensure compliance with all legal, regulatory, contractual, and company requirements.
	Identify operational, financial, and business risks and implement mitigation strategies.
	Oversee implementation of corporate governance and internal control processes.
	Ensure compliance with HSE and quality management standards where applicable.


Job Requirements
Qualifications &amp; Experience:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Engineering, Finance, or a related field.
	Minimum of 10&ndash;15 years of progressive work experience, with at least 5 years in a senior management or leadership role.
	MBA or relevant postgraduate qualification is an added advantage.
	Proven experience within the Oil and gas industry and renewable energy sector, managing multi-functional teams and business operations.
	Experience in business development, strategic planning, and financial management.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strategic planning and execution.
	Leadership and people management.
	Business development and stakeholder management.
	Financial and commercial acumen.
	Operational excellence and process improvement.
	Contract negotiation and relationship management.
	Risk management and decision-making.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and presentation skills.
	Change management and organizational development.


Preferred Certifications:


	MBA or Master&#39;s Degree in a relevant field.
	Project Management Professional (PMP).
	Chartered Management Certifications.
	Relevant industry-specific certifications.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-09",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-20",
  "employmentType": "Contract",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited"
    
  },

General Manager (GM) Job at Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited (formerly Moody International Nigeria Limited) was established in 1996 as a partnership between Moody International Group and Nigerian investors. Since commencing operations in March 1997 the company continues to grow and exerts its presence in numerous Projects in the Nigeria Oil and Gas Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager (GM)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The General Manager is responsible for providing strategic leadership and overall management of the company's operations, business development, project execution, financial performance, and regulatory compliance.
	The role oversees all operational activities within the business units, ensuring projects are delivered safely, efficiently, on schedule, and within budget while driving profitability and business growth.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong leadership experience within the oil & gas industry and renewable energy sector, with a proven track record of managing large-scale projects, business development activities, stakeholder engagement, and multidisciplinary teams.


Key Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership:


	Develop and implement business strategies, plans, and objectives aligned with the organization's vision and goals.
	Drive business growth initiatives, market expansion, and revenue generation opportunities.
	Monitor industry trends, market conditions, and competitor activities to identify business opportunities and risks.
	Provide strategic recommendations to executive management and stakeholders.


Operational Management:


	Oversee daily operations across all departments to ensure efficiency, productivity, and service excellence.
	Establish operational policies, procedures, and performance standards.
	Monitor operational performance and implement continuous improvement initiatives.
	Ensure optimal utilization of company resources and assets.


Financial Management:


	Develop and manage annual budgets, forecasts, and financial plans.
	Monitor financial performance, profitability, and cost-control measures.
	Review financial reports and ensure achievement of revenue and profitability targets.
	Approve expenditures and ensure financial discipline across the organization.


Business Development & Client Relations:


	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, partners, regulatory bodies, and key stakeholders.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities, partnerships, and contracts.
	Lead contract negotiations and major business engagements.
	Ensure high levels of customer satisfaction and service delivery.


People Management:


	Provide leadership, direction, and mentorship to departmental managers and employees.
	Drive employee engagement, performance management, and talent development initiatives.
	Promote a culture of accountability, teamwork, and continuous improvement.
	Oversee workforce planning and organizational development activities.


Compliance, Risk & Governance:


	Ensure compliance with all legal, regulatory, contractual, and company requirements.
	Identify operational, financial, and business risks and implement mitigation strategies.
	Oversee implementation of corporate governance and internal control processes.
	Ensure compliance with HSE and quality management standards where applicable.


Job Requirements
Qualifications & Experience:


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Management, Engineering, Finance, or a related field.
	Minimum of 10–15 years of progressive work experience, with at least 5 years in a senior management or leadership role.
	MBA or relevant postgraduate qualification is an added advantage.
	Proven experience within the Oil and gas industry and renewable energy sector, managing multi-functional teams and business operations.
	Experience in business development, strategic planning, and financial management.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Strategic planning and execution.
	Leadership and people management.
	Business development and stakeholder management.
	Financial and commercial acumen.
	Operational excellence and process improvement.
	Contract negotiation and relationship management.
	Risk management and decision-making.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and presentation skills.
	Change management and organizational development.


Preferred Certifications:


	MBA or Master's Degree in a relevant field.
	Project Management Professional (PMP).
	Chartered Management Certifications.
	Relevant industry-specific certifications.

title: Guest Relations Manager Job at Leonardo Spa Hotel and Suites

  "title": "Network Operations Center (NOC) Operator",
  "description": "IPT PowerTech Group delivers specialized solutions to the power, industrial and telecom sectors in the Middle East, Africa and neighboring countries. Since our inception in Lebanon in 1993, we have grown into a leading group, combining power expertise with telecom infrastructure specialization. Today, we are market leaders in providing specialty batteries and power systems, customized energy efficient/ hybrid and renewable energy solutions, in addition to telecom infrastructure and managed services.

Network Operations Center (NOC) Operator Job at IPT PowerTech Group




        
    

        
        IPT PowerTech Group delivers specialized solutions to the power, industrial and telecom sectors in the Middle East, Africa and neighboring countries. Since our inception in Lebanon in 1993, we have grown into a leading group, combining power expertise with telecom infrastructure specialization. Today, we are market leaders in providing specialty batteries and power systems, customized energy efficient/ hybrid and renewable energy solutions, in addition to telecom infrastructure and managed services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Network Operations Center (NOC) Operator

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Qualifications
